behaviors.description: Command segmentation treats newline-separated statements as separate segments, not one monolithic block.
behaviors.justification: Failure is in deterministic parsing/splitting logic at a single code boundary. No OS/network/subprocess behavior is required, and mocks do not hide this specific failure mode.
behaviors.test_description: Call the segmenter with a multi-line shell string containing assignments and a later command; assert returned segments preserve statement boundaries per line/command separator instead of one combined segment.

behaviors.description: A multi-line shell block with variable assignments followed by `gh pr create` is recognized as a PR-create trigger.
behaviors.justification: The incident came from interaction between segmentation and trigger classification. Testing only classifier logic could miss boundary bugs where upstream segmentation shape changes classification input.
behaviors.test_description: Run the hook classification path end-to-end in-process across real segmentation + trigger-detection modules using a multi-line command block (`VAR=...` lines then `gh pr create ...`), and assert final classification is PR-create trigger (not `not_a_trigger`).

behaviors.description: A similar multi-line block that ends with `gh issue create` is not incorrectly treated as a PR-create trigger.
behaviors.justification: This is the same boundary as behavior 2 but for false-positive prevention. The real risk is module interaction (segmentation + trigger matcher), not external infrastructure.
behaviors.test_description: Execute the same integrated classification path with multi-line assignments followed by `gh issue create ...`; assert classification is non-PR-create and does not enter PR review-loop branch.

behaviors.description: When a PR-create trigger is detected, the hook can reconstruct and persist the PR identifier needed by the review loop state.
behaviors.justification: Behavior crosses parsing/reconstruction logic and persistence boundary (state writer + filesystem/local storage). A pure unit test would miss contract issues between identifier extraction and persisted schema.
behaviors.test_description: Invoke hook handling with a detected PR-create case and representative command/result context; assert a state artifact is written to a temp workspace containing the expected PR identifier field/value consumed by the review loop.

behaviors.description: After PR-create detection, the review gate state is written in a form that the pre-tool enforcement hook can read and enforce.
behaviors.justification: This is an inter-module contract test between writer (PostToolUse) and reader/enforcer (PreToolUse). Real failure appears when modules are wired together over shared state format; no real network/LLM required.
behaviors.test_description: In one test flow, run PostToolUse logic to write state, then invoke PreToolUse enforcement logic against the same temp state location; assert PreToolUse successfully reads the state and applies enforcement decision consistent with an open PR review gate.
